title: Picraena excelsa
intro: Picraena excelsa, also known as the Picrasma tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Picramniaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Africa and Asia. The leaves and bark of the Picrasma tree have been traditionally used in folk medicine, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Picrasma excelsa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, often used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Picraena excelsa is a species of tree that belongs to the family Bignoniaceae. This family consists of over 800 species of flowering plants commonly known as the trumpet vine or catalpa family. Bignoniaceae is further classified under the order Lamiales, which is a large group of flowering plants that also includes mint, snapdragons, and honeysuckles. Members of the Lamiales order are known for their showy flowers and are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Picraena excelsa reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
